mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in Industrial Production (INDPRO) over the past 6 months signals rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past 3 months signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months signals an easing monetary cycle, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in Real Disposable Personal Income (RSAFS) over the past 3 months signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) and a 1% increase in Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) over the past 3 months signal a tightening labor market and rising demand, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
